concept: Function / Perform a System Role or Operation
description: To perform a role or operation within a system. Functioning is the act itself; whether that act serves its intended purpose well, safely, or efficiently is a separate claim with its own evidence.
sound_symbolism: The complete 'keli' (device) begins with crafted precision, while deliberate 'tho' carries the component into its assigned operation. The word ends active without suggesting speed, efficiency, safety, or success beyond the stated function.
grammatical_notes: An intransitive verb: 'ha monaki kelitho.' (This component functions.) A required object or result belongs in a separate clause or compound description. The event-noun rule supplies 'function'; distinct from 'porua' (purpose), 'phoa' (do), 'teru' (process), and 'poru' (productive).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Clear function language supports repairable, understandable infrastructure instead of treating technology as an opaque authority.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Practical knowledge often distinguishes what a part is meant to do from whether it currently does it well, safely, or durably.

tags.activity: performance of a characteristic or required system operation
tags.creation: the role for which a component or system is arranged
